free_text: Experimental study of thick target yield from the
13C(alpha,n0)16C reaction
IPPE 3-MV tandem accelerator
A surface barrier semiconductor detector was
used to analyze the target composition, to control
the target stability, and to determine the carbon
buildup.
Two neutron detectors were used. Angles 0 to
130 deg were covered by a detector located on a
rotating platform while the other detector was
located at a fixed angle of 150 deg. The rotating
neutron detector was based on a p-terphenyl crystal
(40- x 40-mm-diameter cylinder) whereas the other one
was based on a stilbene cylindrical crystal of the
same size. The flight paths from the target to the
neutron detector center were 223.5 and 100 cm,
respectively.
A pellet of amorphous carbon enriched
with 13C pressed into a steel container was used as a
target. The thickness of the carbon layer was ~0.2
mm. The thickness of the container sidewalls was 1 mm,
and the container bottom was 1.5 mm thick.

C-13 content
